metadata.summary: Documentation convention using CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md pairs for multi-agent compatibility. Enables Claude Code and standalone agents to discover context without duplication.

content.title: Project Documentation Convention
content.summary: Convention for organizing documentation so both Claude Code and standalone agents (Agent SDK, subagents, autoheal) can discover and use the same context. Separates essential auto-loaded context (AGENTS.md) from detailed reference (in .agents/) using the @-ref pattern for Claude Code compatibility.

why.p: Claude Code auto-loads `CLAUDE.md` files, but standalone agents (Agent SDK, subagents, autoheal) do not. Putting all context in CLAUDE.md makes it invisible to non-Claude-Code agents. Putting it elsewhere makes Claude Code unaware. The solution partitions documentation into two patterns: (1) essential context accessible to both via AGENTS.md, and (2) detailed reference accessible on-demand via .agents/.

The @-ref pattern allows CLAUDE.md to contain only a single reference line, making it language-agnostic and future-proof. Agents reading AGENTS.md directly get immediate context; Claude Code following the @-ref gets the same content. This eliminates duplication and inconsistency.

list.item: Setting up a new repository or directory that will be worked on by both Claude Code and standalone agents (Agent SDK, autoheal, scheduled workers).
Auditing an existing repository to ensure all directories have discoverable context for multi-agent pipelines.
Onboarding a new project into the faion-network knowledge layer (CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md pair required).
Migrating a project from "all context in CLAUDE.md" to the two-file pattern for agent compatibility.
Trivial directories with <3 files and no logic (e.g., empty `__init__.py` stubs) — skip creating the pair.
External vendored code — do not add CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md to third-party directories.
Temporary scratch directories created during a build or test run — no documentation needed.

section.name: Pattern Overview
section.p: The directory structure uses this pattern:

example.title: Recommended structure
example.code: any-dir/ ├── CLAUDE.md # Always: @AGENTS.md (Claude Code hook, nothing else) ├── AGENTS.md # Essential context for THIS dir (auto-loaded via @-ref) ├── .agents/ # Detailed reference docs (on-demand) │ ├── INDEX.md # Full index of .agents/ contents │ └── topic/ # Subdirs with own CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md └── .product/ # SDD + product docs (per-project) └── INDEX.md # Full index of .product/ contents

section.name: File Roles
section.p: Each file in the convention has a specific purpose:

list.item: `CLAUDE.md` (1 line): Hook for Claude Code — always contains only `@AGENTS.md`. Enables the @-ref pattern.
`AGENTS.md` (20-80 lines): Essential context for this directory. Auto-loaded via @-ref. Self-contained; no @-refs inside.
`.agents/INDEX.md`: Comprehensive documentation index. On-demand reference. Uses table format for easy scanning.
`.product/INDEX.md`: Product/SDD document index. For project-level specs, designs, roadmaps. Workspace root level only.

section.name: How It Works
section.p: The convention enables multi-agent context discovery:

list.item: Claude Code reads `CLAUDE.md` → follows `@AGENTS.md` → gets essential context.
Standalone agents read `AGENTS.md` directly → same essential context without @-ref support.
Either agent reads `.agents/INDEX.md` when they need details (on-demand).
SDD agents read `.product/INDEX.md` for specs, designs, plans.

section.name: AGENTS.md Content Guidelines
section.p: AGENTS.md is the single source of truth for agents entering the directory cold. It should include:

list.item: What this directory IS (one sentence).
Build / test / deploy commands if applicable.
Current state, active gotchas.
Key subdirectory listing.
Links to INDEX.md files so agents know where to look on demand.
File table: `| File | Description |` with one row per file/subdir.
Key types / modules / classes.

section.note: AGENTS.md must be self-contained. It should NOT contain @-refs to other files — standalone agents follow @-refs only if their runtime supports them (Claude Code does; Agent SDK does not).

example.title: INDEX.md example
example.code:
# Index
## Directories
| Directory | Description | |-----------|-------------| | `architecture/` | System design, component diagrams | | `api/` | REST/WS endpoint reference |
## Files
| File | Description | |------|-------------| | `infrastructure.md` | Server topology, ports, domains | | `decisions.md` | Key architectural decisions |

rule.statement: Index tables use one line per entry. No prose in the table itself.
rule.rationale: Agents scan INDEX files for structure; verbose tables slow comprehension.

section.name: Faion-Network Compatibility
section.p: This convention extends (not replaces) the faion-network directory structure:

list.item: `CLAUDE.md` with content → `CLAUDE.md` = `@AGENTS.md`, content → `AGENTS.md`
`.aidocs/` (workspace-level SDD) → Stays as `.aidocs/` at workspace root. Add `INDEX.md`.
`.aidocs/` (per-project SDD) → Renamed to `.product/`. Add `INDEX.md`.
`.aidocs/product_docs/` → `.product/product_docs/` (per-project)
`.aidocs/improvements/` → `.product/improvements/` (per-project)
`.aidocs/memory/` → `.product/memory/` (per-project)
`.claude/skills/` → Unchanged. Skills use own SKILL.md.
`docs/` in repositories → Move to `.agents/` or keep if git-tracked public docs.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md ALWAYS contains only `@AGENTS.md`. No exceptions.
rule.rationale: Any other content breaks the @-ref pattern and makes the file invisible to agents that don't support references.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md is self-contained. No @-refs inside AGENTS.md itself.
rule.rationale: Standalone agents cannot follow @-refs. They must read AGENTS.md and get all necessary context from it directly.

rule.statement: INDEX.md mentions paths, not @-refs (e.g., "see .agents/INDEX.md for details").
rule.rationale: Agents know to look at filesystem paths; they do not auto-follow cross-file references.

rule.statement: Each .agents/ subdir has CLAUDE.md (@AGENTS.md) + AGENTS.md for recursive discovery.
rule.rationale: Enables multi-level directory hierarchies where agents can navigate depth-first through documentation.

rule.statement: Don't create .agents/ for trivial dirs (<3 files, no docs needed).
rule.rationale: Documentation overhead exceeds value for minimal directories.

rule.statement: Don't move code into .agents/. Only documentation and reference material.
rule.rationale: Source code belongs in src/; documentation belongs in .agents/. Keeps separation of concerns.

antipattern.description: If CLAUDE.md contains anything other than `@AGENTS.md`, Claude Code will load mixed content — the @-ref pattern is strict: one line only.
antipattern.reason: Any extra content breaks the pattern. Use `printf "@AGENTS.md\n" > CLAUDE.md` to ensure exact format.

antipattern.description: AGENTS.md with @-refs to other files. Standalone agents do not follow @-refs; they read AGENTS.md directly.
antipattern.reason: AGENTS.md must be fully self-contained. Put cross-references in .agents/INDEX.md instead.

antipattern.description: INDEX.md tables without clear descriptions. Agents scanning for "where is X?" need one-line summaries per entry.
antipattern.reason: Detailed docs go in the referenced files; INDEX.md is only a map.

antipattern.description: Confusing `.product/` (per-project SDD) with `.aidocs/` (workspace-level SDD). Different purposes, different content.
antipattern.reason: Document the mapping explicitly in the root AGENTS.md so agents know which to use.

antipattern.description: Agents creating new directories without creating CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pairs. The pair creation must be explicit.
antipattern.reason: Always include pair creation in agent task prompts. Standard template: CLAUDE.md = `@AGENTS.md`, AGENTS.md = 20-80 lines describing the directory.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: A documentation audit agent (Read, Glob, Grep) scans all directories with source files, checks for CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pairs, and reports missing pairs and content violations. A documentation writer agent (Write, Edit) creates missing files from templates. The audit → write cycle is sequential: no file is created without first checking if it already exists. Human review is recommended before committing the initial pair to a repository.

example.title: Documentation audit script
example.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash # Check every directory with source files has CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md set -euo pipefail ROOT=${1:-.} FAIL=0
find "$ROOT" -type f \( -name "*.py" -o -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.js" -o -name "*.go" \) \ | xargs -I {} dirname {} | sort -u \ | while read -r dir; do [[ "$dir" == */.git/* ]] && continue [[ "$dir" == */node_modules/* ]] && continue [[ "$dir" == */__pycache__/* ]] && continue
if [[ ! -f "$dir/CLAUDE.md" ]]; then echo "MISSING CLAUDE.md: $dir"; FAIL=1 elif ! grep -q "^@AGENTS.md$" "$dir/CLAUDE.md"; then echo "BAD CLAUDE.md (not @-ref only): $dir"; FAIL=1 fi
if [[ ! -f "$dir/AGENTS.md" ]]; then echo "MISSING AGENTS.md: $dir"; FAIL=1 fi done
[[ $FAIL -eq 0 ]] && echo "All directories compliant." || exit 1

rule.statement: Create the CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pair at the same time as the first source file in a new directory.
rule.rationale: Retrofitting documentation is harder and more error-prone than creating it upfront.

rule.statement: Create the CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pair at the same time as the first source file in a new directory.
rule.rationale: Retrofitting documentation is harder and more error-prone than creating it upfront.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md file table format: `| File | Description |` with one row per file/subdir — skip auto-generated files.
rule.rationale: Agents need to know the directory structure; auto-generated files add noise.

rule.statement: `.agents/INDEX.md` table format: `| File/Dir | Description |` — one line per entry, no prose.
rule.rationale: Agents scan INDEX for structure; verbose entries slow comprehension.

rule.statement: Don't put implementation details in AGENTS.md (architecture deep dives, full API reference) — those belong in `.agents/`.
rule.rationale: AGENTS.md is for orientation; detailed docs belong in .agents/ for on-demand reading.

rule.statement: When a directory evolves, update AGENTS.md first — it's the single source of truth for agents entering the directory cold.
rule.rationale: Stale AGENTS.md causes agents to make incorrect decisions based on outdated information.

rule.statement: The convention doesn't replace code comments — it's for cross-agent discoverability, not inline documentation.
rule.rationale: Comments in code are for humans reading the implementation; AGENTS.md is for agents entering the directory without reading code.

section.name: Audit Tools & Scripts
section.p: Verify compliance with the documentation convention across a codebase:

rule.statement: Run compliance audit before committing major documentation changes or onboarding a new directory.
rule.rationale: Catch CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pair issues early; prevents agents from discovering broken documentation.

section.name: Common Directory Patterns
section.p: How the convention applies to different directory types:

list.item: Source directories (src/, lib/, app/) — ALWAYS need CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md with file table and key types
Test directories (tests/, spec/) — CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md if >3 test files; describe test structure
Configuration directories (config/, .github/) — CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md if config is complex; describe env vars and secrets
Documentation directories (docs/, .agents/) — CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md describing the docs structure and navigation
Build/deployment (scripts/, deploy/) — CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md with commands, prerequisites, and failure modes
Package directories (node_modules/, vendor/, .venv/) — NO CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md (vendored code, skip)

section.name: Migration from Old Patterns
section.p: Converting existing projects to the new convention:

list.item: If CLAUDE.md exists with content, create new AGENTS.md with that content, then replace CLAUDE.md with `@AGENTS.md` only
If README.md exists in the directory, move it to `.agents/` and create a minimal AGENTS.md instead
If .aidocs/ exists at project root, rename to .product/ and update all cross-references
Create INDEX.md in .agents/ and .product/ to guide agents through multi-file docs
Update AGENTS.md at each level to mention `.agents/INDEX.md` and `.product/INDEX.md` paths

rule.statement: Migration should be incremental: directory by directory, not all at once.
rule.rationale: Reduces risk of breaking existing workflows; allows testing the new pattern in isolation.
